_provenance.discipline: Process-not-substance. 3R-Critical: Religion + Race + Royalty. Final synthesis applies Stage 3's fact corrections, Stage 2a/2b/2c's bias and framing flags, Stage 4's missing-perspective adds (Anwar's broken pledges, SUARAM half-of-cases finding), and Stage 5's VERIFIED courage flags. Stage 5's MY_OPINION claims NOT imported. Analogy card DROPPED — multiple stages flagged it as misleading and editorialised; CLAUDE.md permits 0 or 1 analogy.

headline: Zahid wants firmer law on insults — sedition cases already up 84% in 2025
context: On 10 May 2026 at the Musyawarah Nasional forum, DPM Zahid called for 'firm, fair, wise' action against insults to Islam and the Malay Rulers, and a strengthened legal framework. CIJ logged Sedition Act cases nearly doubling in 2025.

cards.big: DPM Zahid says insults to Islam and the Malay Rulers need 'firm, fair, wise' action — and a strengthened legal framework.
cards.sub: He spoke 10 May 2026 at the Musyawarah Nasional forum he founded. The laws he wants reinforced — Sedition Act, CMA s.233, Penal Code 298A — are already on the books.
sources: Bernama, 10 May 2026, 'Insults Against Islam, Malay Rulers Must Be Handled Firmly - Ahmad Zahid' (Stage 3 verified)
Malay Mail, 10 May 2026, https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/10/zahid-calls-for-firm-action-against-insults-on-islam-and-malay-rulers-to-safeguard-national-harmony/219419
The Star, 10 May 2026, https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/10/insults-against-islam-malay-rulers-must-be-handled-firmly-says-ahmad-zahid
UMNO Online press release, 10 May 2026

cards.big: At least three statutes already apply: Sedition Act, Penal Code 298A, and CMA s.233 — strengthened to RM500,000 and 'grossly offensive' in February 2025.
cards.sub: The Feb 2025 amendment was a 10x penalty jump. Sedition Act s.3(1)(f) references Article 181 — the Rulers' constitutional position.
sources: Communications and Multimedia (Amendment) Act 2025, Act A1743, s.91 — gazetted 7 Feb 2025, in force 11 Feb 2025; RM50,000 → RM500,000, 1y → 2y jail; substitutes 'grossly offensive' for 'offensive' (Stage 3 verified against AGC primary text): https://lom.agc.gov.my/ilims/upload/portal/akta/outputaktap/2669326_BI/A1743%20BI-%20COMMUNICATIONS%20AND%20MULTIMEDIA%20%28AMENDMENT%29%20ACT%202025.pdf
Sedition Act 1948, s.3(1)(f) — references matters protected by Articles 152, 153, 181 of Federal Constitution (Stage 3 verified): https://www.icnl.org/wp-content/uploads/Malaysia_sedition.pdf
Penal Code Act 574, s.298A (Stage 3 verified): https://www.sprm.gov.my/admin/files/sprm/assets/pdf/penguatkuasaan/Akta_574_%2831.5.2023%29_BI.pdf
Federal Constitution Article 181 (Stage 3 verified)

cards.big: CIJ said Sedition Act cases rose to 36 in 2025 from 19. SUARAM found half of all sedition cases targeted criticism of political leaders.
cards.sub: Across all expression-restricting laws, investigations rose to 233 from 189 (+23%). SUARAM described a state narrative that 'dissent is an act of rudeness'.
sources: Free Malaysia Today, 10 Dec 2025, citing CIJ Freedom of Expression Report 2025: https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2025/12/10/sedition-act-cases-up-84-in-2025-says-cij
CIJ primary press statement, 10 Dec 2025, verifies 233/189 broader figure with 23.28% increase (Stage 3 verified): https://cijmalaysia.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Kenyataan-Akhbar-IHRD-2025.pdf
Malaysiakini, 'Half of sedition cases involve criticism of political leaders — Suaram', citing SUARAM Malaysia Human Rights Report 2025 (Apr 2026): https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/772030
Daily Express citing CIJ figures: http://dailyexpress.com.my/news/272227/sedition-act-cases-up-84-in-2025-says-cij/

cards.big: On 6 February 2026 the apex court restored 'offensive' and 'annoy' in CMA s.233 — but read them down to require clear intent and harm.
cards.sub: Political views that merely cause anger don't trigger the law — but communications attacking the Rulers' role with intent can still fall within it.
sources: Government of Malaysia v Heidy Quah Gaik Li [2026] MLJU 384, delivered 6 Feb 2026. Press summary (Stage 3 verified): https://www.article19.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/GOVERNMENT-OF-MALAYSIA-v-HEIDY-QUAH-GAIK-LI_Press_Summary.pdf

Free Malaysia Today, 6 Feb 2026, 'Online speech restrictions constitutional, rules apex court': https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2026/02/06/online-speech-restrictions-constitutional-rules-apex-court

Malay Mail, 6 Feb 2026, 'Federal Court's pivotal ruling raises bar for prosecuting Malaysians over offensive online remarks': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/02/06/lawyers-federal-courts-pivotal-ruling-raises-bar-for-prosecuting-malaysians-over-offensive-online-remarks/208255
Skrine legal alert March 2026

cards.big: PM Anwar pledged to repeal the Sedition Act in 2018 and again in 2023. The Act remains. The promised replacement is still in draft.
sources: BenarNews, 6 Sept 2023, 'Activists: Anwar has broken promises to repeal Malaysia's restrictive laws': https://www.benarnews.org/english/news/malaysian/dissent-concerns-09062023141252.html

Lawyers for Liberty, 25 Nov 2023, on the PH+Anwar repeal promise: https://www.lawyersforliberty.org/2023/11/25/lfl-the-sedition-charge-against-opposition-politician-razali-idris-is-in-outright-breach-of-ph-and-anwars-promise-to-repeal-the-sedition-act-1948/
Human Rights Watch, 11 Jan 2024, 'Malaysia: Reform Agenda Stalled': https://www.hrw.org/news/2024/01/11/malaysia-reform-agenda-stalled

Amnesty Malaysia, Oct 2025: 'Anwar Ibrahim once promised to end the use of laws like the Sedition Act, but now they're being used to silence those critical of the government': https://www.amnesty.my/2025/10/08/malaysias-assault-on-online-expression-must-end-now/
Ministry of National Unity, 7 Apr 2026, on SKRN bill cabinet review: https://dmedia.penerangan.gov.my/upload/km/07042026.458631609.pdf

cards.big: Parliament raised CMA s.233 fines 10x in Feb 2025. The apex court tightened the law in Feb 2026. The PM has promised repeal since 2018. Zahid asks for more.
sources: Editorial synthesis across the verified findings: Act A1743 (Feb 2025 amendment); Heidy Quah [2026] MLJU 384 (Feb 2026 reinstatement with intent test); Anwar 2018 manifesto + 2023 reiteration of repeal pledge; Zahid 10 May 2026 speech.

revision_history: v2 (Stage 2a correction): Card 4 — added 6 Feb 2026 Federal Court ruling, replaced 'legal limbo' claim.
v3 (Stage 2b correction): Card 1 — added Feb 2025 CMA Amendment (RM500K, 'grossly offensive').

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Context — SUARAM 'a month earlier' framing removed; CIJ Dec 2025 figure substituted. SUARAM 5-year high finding belonged to April 2025 launch about 2024 data, not May 2026 framing.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): Card 2 (Legal) — 'Three statutes criminalise this' → 'At least three statutes already apply'. Statutes have distinct elements.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Card 3 (Rights) — Removed unverified 'highest since 2015 online-speech amendments'. Removed '55% netizens' demographic split (not in CIJ primary press release). Attribution softened to 'CIJ said' / 'SUARAM found'.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Card 4 (Governance) — Replaced 'political speech is excluded; a single post is not enough' with the FC press summary's actual two-pronged framing: political views causing anger are protected, but communications attacking the Rulers' role with intent can still fall within s.233.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Reframe — Replaced '14-year cabinet draft' (which conflated Najib's 2012 Sedition replacement promise with the 2026 SKRN commission bill) with the stronger and unambiguously verified 'Anwar pledged in 2018 + 2023, Act remains, replacement still in draft.'

REPHRASED (Stage 2c bias): Hook — kept Zahid's actual call factually. Hook sub already names the statutes — addresses Stage 2c's 'dismissive' flag by stating context, not characterising the religious anxieties.
ADDED (Stage 4): Card 3 — SUARAM 2025 finding that half of sedition cases target criticism of political leaders. This materially deepens the Rights card without crossing into 3R substance.
ADDED (Stage 4): Reframe — Anwar's 2018 + 2023 pledges, verified via BenarNews + Lawyers for Liberty + HRW + Amnesty Malaysia.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5 verified): Reframe pivots from a Najib-era promise (potential 'conflation' per Stage 3) to the CURRENT PM's broken pledge. This squarely answers Stage 5's 'cross-party complicity' VERIFIED hard truth.

REMOVED (Stage 2c + Stage 3 + Stage 5): Analogy card. Multiple stages flagged it as misleading, editorialised, and overclaiming the FC ruling's scope. CLAUDE.md permits 0 or 1 analogy; 0 is the right answer here. Card count drops from 7 to 6.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): Headline 'sedition cases already up 84% in 2025' — the 84% figure is from CIJ via multiple secondary outlets; safer than the v3 'five-year high' phrasing which was tied to a SUARAM claim Stage 3 found to be timing-misattributed.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): The FC nuance in Card 4 sub — 'communications attacking the Rulers' role with intent can still fall within it' — this is from the FC press summary text Stage 3 cited verbatim and is the most editorially load-bearing addition. Verified.

introduced_claims.claim: Sedition cases already up 84% in 2025 (headline framing)
introduced_claims.primary_source: CIJ Freedom of Expression Report 2025 (Dec 2025), reported by FMT 10 Dec 2025, Daily Express, and The Star 15 Dec 2025; CIJ's own press statement verifies the 233/189 broader basket. The 36-vs-19 Sedition-specific figure is consistently attributed to CIJ across these outlets.

introduced_claims.claim: Card 4 sub: 'Political views that merely cause anger don't trigger the law — but communications attacking the Rulers' role with intent can still fall within it.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Federal Court press summary in Government of Malaysia v Heidy Quah Gaik Li [2026] MLJU 384 (6 Feb 2026), as cited by Stage 3 — both prongs are in the press summary text. This is editorially load-bearing because it reflects the actual two-sided nature of the FC ruling rather than the v3 misleading framing.

introduced_claims.claim: Reframe: 'PM Anwar pledged to repeal the Sedition Act in 2018 and again in 2023.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: PH 2018 manifesto (cited in Lawyers for Liberty press release 25 Nov 2023); Anwar's July 2023 statement reiterating intent (BenarNews 6 Sept 2023; HRW 11 Jan 2024 reporting on PMX's broken commitments).

introduced_claims.claim: View: 'The PM has promised repeal since 2018.'

synthesis_notes: OVER-INTEGRATION decisions: (1) Stage 2c said omit specific provocations was bias; Stage 5 said hiding behind 3R was cowardice; Stage 4 said add the substantive triggers. T4A's 3R Religion+Royalty discipline requires we do NOT amplify specific allegedly-insulting content — naming the 'Allah' issue or 'Sultan of Selangor' provocations would itself amplify them and violate the no-substance rule. I chose Stage 1's discipline over Stage 2c/4/5's pressure. (2) Stage 5 alleged cowardice for not naming cross-ethnic asymmetric enforcement; Stage 5 itself labelled those claims MY_OPINION not VERIFIED. Did not import. (3) Stage 4 surfaced East Malaysia (Bajau Laut, UMS Sabah) cases, B40 class dimension, gender dimension — all valid but tangential to this specific 'firmer law vs existing landscape' story arc. Did not add; flagged as follow-up issue material. (4) Stage 3 said the '14-year cabinet draft' framing conflated the 2012 Najib Sedition replacement promise with the April 2026 SKRN commission bill — and the period is ~13y10m, not 14y. Rather than soften, I pivoted the reframe entirely to Anwar's 2018+2023 pledges, which (a) sidesteps the conflation, (b) targets the CURRENT PM specifically, (c) is unambiguously verified across BenarNews + LFL + HRW + Amnesty. Stronger story, fewer factual exposure points. (5) ANALOGY DROP: Stage 2c said delete; Stage 3 flagged overclaim of FC ruling scope; Stage 5 called it the weakest card. Three stages converging on 'drop' is overwhelming. Issue moves from 7 cards to 6. COMPRESSION decisions: Card 3 dropped '42 individuals' and '55% netizens' because Stage 3 couldn't verify these from CIJ's primary press release — kept only what's anchored. Card 4's 'narrow test of repeat, intentional, harmful conduct' tightened to 'clear intent and harm' to avoid Stage 3's overclaim flag on the full list.
sr_score_rationale: Synthesis quality: Stage 3 corrections all applied with explicit revision-history tagging. Stage 2a/2b/2c bias flags addressed (analogy dropped; CMA penalty figures cited to AGC primary; 'three statutes criminalise' softened to 'at least three statutes apply'). Stage 4's two highest-value adds incorporated (Anwar pledges, SUARAM half-of-cases). Stage 5's VERIFIED items reflected; MY_OPINION items declined. 3R discipline preserved. Capped at 88 (not 90+) because: (a) the headline's 84% figure rests on CIJ secondary attribution rather than the CIJ FoE Report 2025 PDF directly (Stage 3 located the press release but not the full report PDF) — Stage 6 self-verified across multiple outlets but the primary remains the report itself; (b) the FC nuance in Card 4 sub relies on Stage 3's reading of the FC press summary, not on the full judgment from e-Kehakiman; (c) the reframe's '2023' references to Anwar's reiteration are via Amnesty/BenarNews/HRW rather than a direct PMO transcript.

_self_check.overclaim_check: We do NOT assert that more enforcement is wrong, or that Zahid's call is wrong, or that any specific case was wrongly charged. The FC ruling in Card 4 is reported both ways (reinstated AND read down). The Rulers reference in Card 4 sub is the FC's own statement, not editorial speculation. The reframe quotes Anwar's broken pledge — verifiable as broken because the law remains. The view does not editorialise about whether Zahid's call should succeed.
_self_check.underclaim_check: Sedition charges DID rise 84%. SUARAM DID find half target political leaders. The FC DID reinstate the words and DID impose a narrow intent test. Anwar DID pledge repeal in 2018 and 2023, and the Act IS still on the books. None softened.
_self_check.misleading_framing_check: Headline leads with verified CIJ 84% figure. Card 1 establishes the legal stack and recent strengthening BEFORE the rights numbers. Card 4 reports both sides of the FC ruling (reinstated + narrowed). Reframe targets the CURRENT PM, not just Najib's older promise. View synthesises without endorsing either side's policy preference.
_self_check.unverified_detail_check: Every specific traces back to a verifying stage or Stage 6 self-verification: speech facts (Stage 3 + Bernama), CMA amendment penalty and date (Stage 3 verified against Act A1743), Sedition Act references (Stage 3 verified), CIJ 36-vs-19 and 233-vs-189 (Stage 3 verified the basket; secondary outlets cite the Sedition-specific figure to CIJ), FC ruling date + reinstatement + intent test (Stage 3 verified via press summary), SUARAM half-of-cases (Stage 6 self-verified via Malaysiakini citing SUARAM HRR 2025), Anwar 2018+2023 pledges (Stage 6 self-verified via BenarNews + Lawyers for Liberty + HRW + Amnesty).
_self_check.3R_special_caution: RELIGION: We do not characterise Islam, Muslim views, or the substance of any allegedly insulting speech. Specific cases (Ampang azan, Koran desecration, 'Allah socks') deliberately unnamed. The 'halal', 'Allah', 'Sultan of Selangor' provocations Zahid cited in his speech are NOT named in the cards — naming them amplifies the underlying alleged speech and is not necessary to the legal-mechanism story. Stage 2c flagged this as bias; we maintain it as discipline. RACE: 'Malay' sentiment not treated as monolith; we report Zahid spoke at a forum themed 'Malay Unease' without endorsing or dismissing the framing. ROYALTY: The Card 4 sub mentions the Rulers — only to report the Federal Court's own statutory characterisation that communications attacking the Rulers' constitutional role with intent can fall within s.233. This is the FC's text, not editorial speculation. We do not characterise what the Rulers think, want, or have said. No public Conference of Rulers statement on Zahid's 10 May 2026 speech has been identified.
_self_check.process_not_people_check: All sharp claims target structures: existing statutes, the Feb 2025 amendment, the enforcement trend, the Feb 2026 court ruling, the bill in draft, the 2018+2023 PM pledges. Zahid is named for what he said in his official DPM capacity. Anwar is named for the verifiable pledges he made and has not delivered. No person is characterised as bad-faith. The reframe targets the gap between pledge and delivery, not the individuals.
